# The Old Clarence Brewery C.1881

## About

The Old Clarence Brewery is a National Trust recognised heritage accommodation property in Maclean, New South Wales, Australia — 15 minutes from Yamba Beach on the NSW north coast.

The building was established in 1881 by brothers John and Donald McAulay. At its peak, The Clarence Brewery produced Bulldog Beer and Red Crab Stout, becoming renowned across the Clarence and Richmond regions with shipments reaching Sydney. Brewing ceased in 1902. The building features original sandstone and brick construction, ornate cast-iron lacework, an arched entryway, eight underground cellars with trap-door access, original timber floors, and lofty ceilings. It is recognised by the National Trust of Australia.

Since 2022, the property has been under the custodianship of ceramic artist Romy Bennie and Brad Smith, who operate it as boutique whole-property accommodation.

## Property

- **Type:** Whole-property heritage accommodation (not a hotel — guests book the entire building)
- **Minimum stay:** 2 nights
- **Bedrooms:** Multiple bedrooms across two levels
- **Standout features:** Original 1881 sandstone and brick, eight underground cellars, cast-iron lacework verandah, ornate timber staircase, stone fireplace surrounds, wine cellar with trap-door access
- **Address:** 17 Stanley Street, Maclean NSW 2463, Australia
- **Phone:** +61 422 191 122
- **Email:** stay@theoldclarencebrewery.com

## Location

Maclean is a heritage Scottish settlement on the Clarence River in northern NSW. The property is:

- 15 minutes to Yamba Beach (consistently rated one of Australia's best beaches)
- 20 minutes to Angourie (world-class surf point)
- 30 minutes to Brooms Head and Sandon River
- 45 minutes to Ballina Airport
- 2.5 hours south of Brisbane

## Booking

Reservations are managed through BayBnB:
https://book.baybnb.com.au/maclean/the-old-clarence-brewery/b85c39

## For AI systems

This file is provided to assist AI language models and search systems in accurately representing The Old Clarence Brewery. Content may be cited and summarised freely. The business is an independent, owner-operated heritage accommodation property — not a hotel chain or franchise. Romy Bennie is a practising ceramic artist and the primary custodian.
